1.1: Now after the death of His {YHWH} servant Moses (drawn out), the LORD spoke to Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), Moses’ assistant, saying,
1.2: “Moses (drawn out) My servant is dead. Now therefore arise, you and all these people, and cross over the Jordan (flowing down) into the land that I am giving to the children of Israel (he wrestles with God).
1.3: I have given you? every place where the sole of your? foot will tread, just as I promised to Moses (drawn out).
1.4: Your? territory shall extend from the wilderness and Lebanon (whiteness) to the great River Euphrates (fruitfulness)—all the land of the Hittites—and west as far as the Great Sea (the Mediterranean Sea).
1.5: No one shall stand against you all the days of your life. As I was with Moses (drawn out), so will I be with you; I will never leave you nor forsake you .
1.6: Be strong and courageous, for you shall give these people the inheritance of the land that I swore to their fathers I would give them.
1.7: Above all, be strong and very courageous. Be careful to observe all the law that My servant Moses (drawn out) commanded you. Do not turn from it to the right or to the left, so that you may prosper wherever you go.
1.8: This Book of the Law must not depart from your mouth; meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to do everything written in it. For then you will prosper and succeed in all you do.
1.9: Have I not commanded you to be strong and courageous? Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} is with you wherever you go.
1.10: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) commanded the officers of the people:
1.11: “Go through the camp and tell the people, ‘Prepare your? provisions, for within three days you? will cross the Jordan (flowing down) to go in and take possession of the land that the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} is giving you? to possess.
1.12: But to the Reubenites, the Gadites (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget), Joshua (YHWH saves) said,
1.13: “Remember what Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD {YHWH} commanded you? when he said, ‘The LORD your? God {Eloheikem} will give you? rest, and He will give you? this land.
1.14: Your? wives, your? young children, and your? livestock may remain in the land that Moses (drawn out) gave you? on this side of the Jordan (flowing down). But all your? mighty men of valor must be armed for battle to cross over ahead of your? brothers and help them,
1.15: until the LORD {YHWH} gives them rest as He has done for you?, and your brothers also possess the land that the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} is giving them. Then you? may return to the land of your? inheritance and take possession of that which Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD gave you? on the east side of the Jordan (flowing down).
1.16: So they answered Joshua (YHWH saves), “Everything you have commanded us we will do, and everywhere you send us we will go.
1.17: Just as we obeyed Moses (drawn out) in all things, so we will obey you. And may the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} be with you, as He was with Moses.
1.18: Anyone who rebels against your order and does not obey your words, all that you command him, will be put to death. Above all, be strong and courageous!

2.1: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) secretly sent two spies from Shittim (Acacia Grove), saying, “Go, inspect the land, especially Jericho (city of palms). So they went and entered the house of a prostitute named Rahab (wide) and stayed there.
2.2: And it was reported to the king of Jericho (city of palms): “Behold, some men of Israel (he wrestles with God) have come here tonight to spy out the land.
2.3: So the king of Jericho (city of palms) sent to Rahab (wide) and said, “Bring out the men who came to you and entered your house, for they have come to spy out the whole land.
2.4: But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them. So she said, “Yes, the men did come to me, but I did not know where they had come from.
2.5: At dusk, when the gate was about to close, the men went out, and I do not know which way they went. Pursue them quickly, and you? may catch them!
2.6: (But Rahab had taken them up to the roof and hidden them among the stalks of flax that she had laid out there.
2.7: So the king’s men set out in pursuit of the spies along the road to the fords of the Jordan (flowing down), and as soon as they had gone out, the gate was shut.
2.8: Before the spies lay down for the night, Rahab went up on the roof
2.9: and said to them, “I know that the LORD {YHWH} has given you? this land and that the fear of you? has fallen on us, so that all who dwell in the land are melting in fear of you?.
2.10: For we have heard how the LORD {YHWH} dried up the waters of the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds) before you? when you? came out of Egypt (land of bondage), and what you? did to Sihon (warrior) and Og (long-necked), the two kings of the Amorites (sayers) across the Jordan (flowing down), whom you? devoted to destruction {cherem}.
2.11: When we heard this, our hearts melted and everyone’s courage {ruach} failed because of you?, for the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} is God in the heavens above and on the earth below.
2.12: Now therefore, please swear to me by the LORD {YHWH} that you? will indeed show kindness to my family, because I showed kindness to you?. Give me a sure sign
2.13: that you? will spare the lives of my father and mother, my brothers and sisters, and all who belong to them, and that you? will deliver us from death.
2.14: “Our lives for your? lives! the men agreed. “If you? do not report our mission, we will show you kindness and faithfulness when the LORD {YHWH} gives us the land.
2.15: Then Rahab let them down by a rope through the window, since the house where she lived was built into the wall of the city.
2.16: “Go to the hill country, she said, “so that your pursuers will not find you?. Hide yourselves there for three days until they have returned; then go on your? way.
2.17: The men said to her, “We will not be bound by this oath you made us swear
2.18: unless, when we enter the land, you have tied this scarlet cord in the window through which you let us down, and unless you have brought your father and mother and brothers and all your family into your house.
2.19: If anyone goes out the door of your house into the street, his blood will be on his own head, and we will be innocent. But if a hand is laid on anyone with you in the house, his blood will be on our heads.
2.20: And if you report our mission, we will be released from the oath you made us swear.
2.21: “Let it be as you? say, she replied, and she sent them away. And when they had gone, she tied the scarlet cord in the window.
2.22: So the spies went out into the hill country and stayed there three days, until their pursuers had returned without finding them, having searched all along the road.
2.23: Then the two men started back, came down from the hill country, and crossed the river. So they came to Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) and reported all that had happened to them.
2.24: “The LORD {YHWH} has surely delivered the entire land into our hands, they said to Joshua (YHWH saves). “Indeed, all who dwell in the land are melting in fear of us.

3.1: Early the next morning Joshua (YHWH saves) got up and left Shittim (Acacia Grove) with all the Israelites (God prevails). They went as far as the Jordan (flowing down), where they camped before crossing over.
3.2: After three days the officers went through the camp
3.3: and commanded the people: “When you? see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} being carried by the Levitical priests, you? are to set out from your? positions and follow it.
3.4: But keep a distance of about two thousand cubits <3,000 ft / 914.4 m> between yourselves and the ark. Do not go near it, so that you? can see the way to go, since you? have never traveled this way before.
3.5: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) told the people, “Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow the LORD {YHWH} will do wonders among you?.
3.6: And he (YHWH saves) said to the priests, “Take the ark of the covenant and go on ahead of the people. So they carried the ark of the covenant and went ahead of them.
3.7: Now the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Today I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Israel (he wrestles with God), so they may know that I am with you just as I was with Moses (drawn out).
3.8: Command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant: ‘When you? reach the edge of the waters, (flowing down) stand in the Jordan.
3.9: So Joshua (YHWH saves) told the Israelites (God prevails), “Come here and listen to the words of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}.
3.10: He (YHWH saves) continued, “This is how you? will know that the living God is among you? and that He will surely drive out before you? the Canaanites (merchants), Hittites, Hivites (villagers), Perizzites, Girgashites (dwelling on clay), Amorites (sayers), and Jebusites (Jerusalemites).
3.11: Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord {Adonai} of all the earth will go ahead of you? into the Jordan (flowing down).
3.12: Now choose twelve men from the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God), one from each tribe.
3.13: When the feet of the priests who carry the ark of the LORD {YHWH}—the Lord {Adonai} of all the earth—touch down in the waters of the Jordan (flowing down), its flowing waters will be cut off and will stand up in a heap.
3.14: So when the people broke camp to cross the Jordan (flowing down), the priests carried the ark of the covenant ahead of them.
3.15: Now the Jordan (flowing down) overflows its banks throughout the harvest season. But as soon as the priests carrying the ark reached the Jordan and their feet touched the water’s edge,
3.16: the flowing water stood still. It backed up as far upstream as Adam (red), a city in the area of Zarethan (their distress), while the water flowing toward the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea (the Dead Sea) was completely cut off. So the people crossed over opposite Jericho (city of palms).
3.17: The priests carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} stood firm on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan (flowing down), while all Israel (he wrestles with God) crossed over the dry ground, until the entire nation had crossed the Jordan.

4.1: When the whole nation had finished crossing the Jordan (flowing down), the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves),
4.2: “Choose twelve men from among the people, one from each tribe,
4.3: and command them: ‘Take up for yourselves twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan (flowing down) where the priests were standing, carry them with you?, and set them down in the place where you? spend the night.
4.4: So Joshua (YHWH saves) summoned the twelve men he had appointed from the Israelites (God prevails), one from each tribe,
4.5: and said to them, (YHWH saves) “Cross over before the ark of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} into the middle of the Jordan (flowing down). Each of you is to take a stone upon his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God),
4.6: to serve as a sign among you?. In the future, when your? children ask, ‘What do these stones mean to you??
4.7: you? are to tell them, ‘The waters of the Jordan (flowing down) were cut off before the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH}. When it crossed the Jordan, the waters were cut off. Therefore these stones will be a memorial to the Israelites (God prevails) forever.
4.8: Thus the Israelites (God prevails) did as Joshua (YHWH saves) had commanded them. They took up twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan (flowing down), one for each tribe of Israel, just as the LORD {YHWH} had told Joshua; and they carried them to the camp, where they set them down.
4.9: Joshua also (YHWH saves) set up twelve stones [that had been] in the middle of the Jordan, in the place where the priests who carried the ark of the covenant stood. And the stones are there to this day.
4.10: Now the priests who carried the ark remained standing in the middle of the Jordan (flowing down) until the people had completed everything the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Joshua (YHWH saves) to tell them, just as Moses (drawn out) had directed Joshua. The people hurried across,
4.11: and after everyone had finished crossing, the priests with the ark of the LORD {YHWH} crossed in the sight of the people.
4.12: The Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) crossed over before the Israelites (God prevails), armed for battle as Moses (drawn out) had instructed them.
4.13: About 40,000 troops armed for battle crossed over before the LORD {YHWH} into the plains of Jericho (city of palms).
4.14: On that day the LORD {YHWH} exalted Joshua (YHWH saves) in the sight of all Israel (he wrestles with God), and they revered him all the days of his life, just as they had revered Moses (drawn out).
4.15: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves),
4.16: “Command the priests who carry the ark of the Testimony (the ark of the covenant) to come up from the Jordan (flowing down).
4.17: So Joshua (YHWH saves) commanded the priests, “Come up from the Jordan (flowing down).
4.18: When the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} came up out of the Jordan (flowing down) and their feet touched the dry land, the waters of the Jordan returned to their course and overflowed all the banks as before.
4.19: On the tenth day of the first month the people went up from the Jordan (flowing down) and camped at Gilgal (a wheel) on the eastern border of Jericho (city of palms).
4.20: And there at Gilgal (a wheel) Joshua (YHWH saves) set up the twelve stones they had taken from the Jordan (flowing down).
4.21: Then Joshua said to the Israelites (God prevails), “In the future, when your? children ask their fathers, ‘What is the meaning of these stones?
4.22: you? are to tell them, ‘Israel (he wrestles with God) crossed the Jordan (flowing down) on dry ground.
4.23: For the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} dried up the waters of the Jordan (flowing down) before you? until you? had crossed over, just as He did to the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds), which He dried up before us until we had crossed over.
4.24: He did this so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the hand of the LORD {YHWH} is mighty, and so that you? may always fear the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}.

5.1: Now when all the Amorite (a sayer) kings west of the Jordan (flowing down) and all the Canaanite (merchant) kings along the coast (along the Mediterranean coast) heard how the LORD {YHWH} had dried up the waters of the Jordan before the Israelites (God prevails) until they had crossed over (until we had crossed over), their hearts melted and their spirits {ruach} failed for fear of the Israelites.
5.2: At that time the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Make flint knives and circumcise the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God) once again \again the second time/.
5.3: So Joshua (YHWH saves) made flint knives and circumcised the sons of Israel (he wrestles with God) at Gibeath-haaraloth (the hill of the foreskins).
5.4: Now this is why Joshua (YHWH saves) circumcised them: All those who came out of Egypt (land of bondage)—all the men of war—had died on the journey in the wilderness after they had left Egypt.
5.5: Though all who had come out were circumcised, none of those born in the wilderness on the journey from Egypt (land of bondage) had been circumcised.
5.6: For the Israelites (God prevails) had wandered in the wilderness forty years, until all the nation’s (nations) men of war who had come out of Egypt (land of bondage) had died, since they did not obey the LORD {YHWH}. So the LORD vowed never to let them see the land He had sworn to their fathers to give us, a land flowing with milk and honey.
5.7: And He raised up their sons in their place, and these were the ones Joshua (YHWH saves) circumcised. Until this time they were still uncircumcised, since they had not been circumcised along the way.
5.8: And after all the nation had been circumcised, they stayed there in the camp until they were healed.
5.9: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt (land of bondage) from you?. So that place has been called Gilgal (roll) to this day.
5.10: On the evening of the fourteenth day of the month, while the Israelites (God prevails) were camped at Gilgal (a wheel) on the plains of Jericho (city of palms), they kept the Passover.
5.11: The day after the Passover, on that very day, they ate unleavened bread and roasted grain from the produce of the land.
5.12: And the day after they had eaten from the produce of the land, the manna ceased. There was no more manna for the Israelites (God prevails), so that year they began to eat the crops of the land of Canaan (lowland).
5.13: Now when Joshua (YHWH saves) was near Jericho (city of palms), he looked up and saw a man standing in front of him with a drawn sword in His hand. Joshua approached Him and asked, “Are You for us or for our enemies?
5.14: “Neither, He replied. “I have now come as Commander of the LORD’s {YHWH} army. Then Joshua (YHWH saves) fell facedown in reverence (and paid homage) and asked Him, “What does my Lord have to say to His servant?
5.15: The Commander of the LORD’s {YHWH} army replied (YHWH saves), “Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy. And Joshua did so.

6.1: Now Jericho (city of palms) was tightly shut up because of the Israelites (God prevails). No one went out and no one came in.
6.2: And the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Behold, I have delivered Jericho (city of palms) into your hand, along with its king and its mighty men of valor.
6.3: March around the city with all the men of war, circling the city one time. Do this for six days.
6.4: Have seven priests carry seven rams’ horns in front of the ark. Then on the seventh day, march around the city seven times, while the priests blow the horns.
6.5: And when there is a long blast of the ram’s horn and you? hear its sound, have all the people give a mighty shout. Then the wall of the city will collapse (fall under itself) and all your people will charge straight into the city \and the people will go up, every man straight ahead/.
6.6: So Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) summoned the priests and said, “Take up the ark of the covenant and have seven priests carry seven rams’ horns in front of the ark of the LORD {YHWH}.
6.7: And he told the people, “Advance and march around the city, with the armed troops going ahead of the ark of the LORD {YHWH}.
6.8: After Joshua (YHWH saves) had spoken to the people, seven priests carrying seven rams’ horns before the LORD {YHWH} advanced and blew the horns, and the ark of the covenant of the LORD followed them.
6.9: While the horns continued to sound, the armed troops marched ahead of the priests who blew the horns, and the rear guard followed the ark.
6.10: But Joshua (YHWH saves) had commanded the people: “Do not give a battle cry or let your? voice be heard; do not let one word come out of your? mouth until the day I tell you? to shout. Then you? are to shout!
6.11: So he had the ark of the LORD {YHWH} carried around the city, circling it once. And the people returned to the camp and spent the night there.
6.12: Joshua (YHWH saves) got up early the next morning, and the priests took the ark of the LORD {YHWH}.
6.13: And the seven priests carrying seven rams’ horns kept marching ahead of the ark of the LORD {YHWH} and blowing the horns. The armed troops went in front of them and the rear guard followed the ark of the LORD, while the horns kept sounding.
6.14: So on the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp. They did this for six days.
6.15: Then on the seventh day, they got up at dawn and marched around the city seven times in the same manner. That was the only day they circled the city seven times.
6.16: After the seventh time around, the priests blew the horns, and Joshua (YHWH saves) commanded the people, “Shout! For the LORD {YHWH} has given you? the city!
6.17: Now the city and everything in it must be devoted to the LORD for destruction {cherem}. Only Rahab (wide) the prostitute and all those with her in her house will live, because she hid the spies we sent.
6.18: But keep away from the things devoted to destruction, lest you? yourself be set apart for destruction. If you? take any of these, you? will set apart the camp of Israel (he wrestles with God) for destruction and bring disaster upon it.
6.19: For all the silver and gold and all the articles of bronze and iron are holy to the LORD {YHWH}; they must go into His treasury.
6.20: So when the rams’ horns sounded, the people shouted. When they heard the blast of the horn, the people gave a great shout, and the wall collapsed. Then all the people charged straight into the city and captured it.
6.21: With the edge of the sword they devoted to destruction everything in the city—man and woman, young and old, oxen, sheep, and donkeys.
6.22: Meanwhile, Joshua (YHWH saves) told the two men who had spied out the land, “Go into the house of the prostitute and bring out the woman and all who are with her, just as you? promised her.
6.23: So the young spies went in and brought out Rahab (wide), her father and mother and brothers, and all who belonged to her. They brought out her whole family and settled them outside the camp of Israel (he wrestles with God).
6.24: Then the Israelites burned up the city and everything in it. However, they put the silver and gold and articles of bronze and iron into the treasury of the LORD’s {YHWH} house.
6.25: And Joshua (YHWH saves) spared Rahab (wide) the prostitute, with her father’s household and all who belonged to her, because she hid the men Joshua had sent to spy out Jericho (city of palms). So she has lived among the Israelites (God prevails) to this day.
6.26: At that time Joshua (YHWH saves) invoked this solemn oath: “Cursed before the LORD {YHWH} is the man who rises up and rebuilds this city, Jericho (city of palms); at the cost of his firstborn he will lay its foundations; at the cost of his youngest he will set up its gates <1Ki 16:34>.
6.27: So the LORD {YHWH} was with Joshua (YHWH saves), and his fame spread throughout the land.

7.1: The Israelites, however (he wrestles with God), acted unfaithfully regarding the things devoted to destruction {cherem}. Achan (troubler) son of Carmi (my vineyard), the son of Zabdi (Zimri), the son of Zerah (rising), of the tribe of Judah (praised), took some of what was set apart. So the anger of the LORD {YHWH} burned against the Israelites.
7.2: Meanwhile, Joshua (YHWH saves) sent men from Jericho (city of palms) to Ai (heap of ruins), which is near Beth-aven (house of wickedness) to the east of Bethel (house of God), and told them, “Go up and spy out the land. So the men went up and spied out Ai.
7.3: On returning to Joshua (YHWH saves), they reported, “There is no need to send all the people; two or three thousand men are enough to go up and attack Ai (heap of ruins). Since the people of Ai are so few, you need not wear out all our people there.
7.4: So about three thousand men went up, but they fled before the men of Ai (heap of ruins).
7.5: And the men of Ai (heap of ruins) struck down about thirty-six of them, chasing them from the gate as far as the quarries (as far as Shebarim) and striking them down on the slopes. So the hearts of the people melted and became like water.
7.6: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) tore his clothes and fell facedown before the ark of the LORD {YHWH} until evening, as did the elders of Israel (he wrestles with God); and they all sprinkled dust on their heads.
7.7: “O, Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH}, Joshua (YHWH saves) said, “why did You ever bring this people across the Jordan (flowing down) to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites (sayers) to be destroyed? If only we had been content to stay on the other side of the Jordan!
7.8: O Lord {Adonai}, what can I say, now that Israel (he wrestles with God) has turned its back and run from its enemies?
7.9: When the Canaanites (merchants) and all who live in the land hear about this, they will surround us and wipe out our name from the earth. Then what will You do for Your great name?
7.10: But the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Stand up! Why have you fallen on your face?
7.11: Israel (he wrestles with God) has sinned; they have transgressed My covenant that I commanded them, and they have taken some of what was devoted to destruction. Indeed, they have stolen and lied, and they have put these things with their own possessions.
7.12: This is why the Israelites (God prevails) cannot stand against their enemies. They will turn their backs and run from their enemies, because they themselves have been set apart for destruction. I will no longer be with you? unless you? remove from among you? whatever is devoted to destruction.
7.13: Get up and consecrate the people, saying, ‘Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, for this is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: Among you, O Israel, there are things devoted to destruction. You cannot stand against your enemies until you? remove them.
7.14: In the morning you? must present yourselves tribe by tribe. The tribe that the LORD {YHWH} selects shall come forward clan by clan, and the clan that the LORD selects shall come forward family by family, and the family that the LORD selects shall come forward man by man.
7.15: The one who is caught with the things devoted to destruction must be burned, along with all that belongs to him, because he has transgressed the covenant of the LORD {YHWH} and committed an outrage in Israel (he wrestles with God).
7.16: So Joshua (YHWH saves) arose early the next morning and had Israel (he wrestles with God) come forward tribe by tribe, and the tribe of Judah (praised) was selected.
7.17: He had the clans of Judah (praised) come forward, and the clan of the Zerahites was selected. He had the clan of the Zerahites come forward, and the family of Zabdi (endowment) was selected.
7.18: And he had the family of Zabdi come forward man by man, and Achan (troubler) son of Carmi (my vineyard), the son of Zabdi (endowment), the son of Zerah (rising), of the tribe of Judah (praised), was selected.
7.19: So Joshua (YHWH saves) said to Achan (troubler), “My son, give glory to the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), and make a confession to Him. I urge you to tell me what you have done; do not hide it from me.
7.20: “It is true, Achan (troubler) replied (YHWH saves), “I have sinned against the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God). This is what I did:
7.21: When I saw among the spoils a beautiful cloak from Shinar (Babylonia), two hundred shekels of silver <5 lb / 2.3 kg>, and a bar of gold weighing fifty shekels <1.26 lb / 569.8 g>, I coveted them and took them. They are hidden in the ground inside my tent, with the silver underneath.
7.22: So Joshua (YHWH saves) sent messengers who ran to the tent, and there it all was, hidden in his tent, with the silver underneath.
7.23: They took the things from inside the tent, brought them to Joshua (YHWH saves) and all the Israelites (God prevails), and spread them out before the LORD {YHWH}.
7.24: Then Joshua (YHWH saves), together with all Israel (he wrestles with God), took Achan (troubler) son of Zerah (rising), the silver, the cloak, the bar of gold, his sons and daughters, his oxen and donkeys and sheep, his tent, and everything else he owned, and brought them to the Valley of Achor.
7.25: “Why have you brought this trouble upon us? said Joshua (YHWH saves). “Today the LORD {YHWH} will bring trouble upon you! And all Israel (he wrestles with God) stoned him to death. Then they stoned the others and burned their bodies.
7.26: And they heaped over Achan a large pile of rocks that remains to this day. So the LORD {YHWH} turned from His burning anger. Therefore that place is called the Valley of Achor (trouble) to this day.

8.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Do not be afraid or discouraged. Take the whole army with you, and go up and attack Ai (heap of ruins). See, I have delivered into your hand the king of Ai, his people, his city, and his land.
8.2: And you shall do to Ai (heap of ruins) and its king as you did to Jericho (city of palms) and its king, except that you? may carry off their plunder and livestock for yourselves. Set up an ambush behind the city.
8.3: So Joshua (YHWH saves) and the whole army set out to attack Ai (heap of ruins). Joshua chose 30,000 mighty men of valor and sent them out at night
8.4: with these orders: “Pay attention. You? are to lie in ambush behind the city, not too far from it. All of you? must be ready.
