13.3: from the Shihor (dark) east of Egypt (land of bondage) to the territory of Ekron (torn up by the roots) on the north (considered to be Canaanite territory (merchants))—that of the five Philistine (immigrants) rulers of Gaza, Ashdod (I will spoil), Ashkelon (the fire of infamy), Gath (belonging to Gath), and Ekron, as well as that of the Avvites (ruins);
13.4: to the south, all the land of the Canaanites (merchants), from Mearah (Arah) of the Sidonians to Aphek (enclosure), as far as the border of the Amorites (sayers);
13.5: the land of the Gebalites (the area of Byblos); and all Lebanon (whiteness) to the east, from Baal-gad (lord of fortune) below Mount Hermon (a sanctuary) to Lebo-hamath (fortress).
13.6: All the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon (whiteness) to Misrephoth-maim (burnings of water)—all the Sidonians—I Myself will drive out before the Israelites (God prevails). Be sure to divide it by lot as an inheritance to Israel, as I have commanded you.
13.7: Now therefore divide this land as an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget).
13.8: The other half of Manasseh, along with the Reubenites and Gadites (a troop / fortune), had received the inheritance Moses (drawn out) had given them beyond the Jordan (flowing down) to the east, just as Moses the servant of the LORD {YHWH} had assigned to them:
13.9: The area from Aroer (ruins) on the rim of the Arnon (rushing stream) Valley, along with the city in the middle of the valley, the whole plateau of Medeba (water of rest) as far as Dibon (wasting),
13.10: and all the cities of Sihon (warrior) king of the Amorites (sayers) who reigned in Heshbon (stronghold), as far as the border of the Ammonites (tribal);
13.11: also Gilead (rocky region) and the territory of the Geshurites and Maacathites (she has pressed), all of Mount Hermon (a sanctuary), and all Bashan (fruitful) as far as Salecah (migration)—
13.12: the whole kingdom of Og (long-necked) in Bashan (fruitful), who had reigned in Ashtaroth (star) and Edrei (good pasture) and had remained as a remnant of the Rephaim. Moses (drawn out) had struck them down and dispossessed them,
13.13: but the Israelites (God prevails) did not drive out the Geshurites or the Maacathites (she has pressed). So Geshur (proud beholder) and Maacath (oppression) dwell among the Israelites to this day.
13.14: To the tribe of Levi (joined to), however, Moses had given no inheritance. The food offerings to the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), are their inheritance, just as He had promised them.
13.15: This is what Moses (drawn out) had given to the clans of the tribe of Reuben (behold a son):
13.16: The territory from Aroer (ruins) on the rim of the Arnon (rushing stream) Valley, along with the city in the middle of the valley, to the whole plateau beyond Medeba (water of rest),
13.17: to Heshbon (stronghold) and all its cities on the plateau, including Dibon (wasting), Bamoth-baal (high places of Baal), Beth-baal-meon (house of Baal),
13.19: Kiriathaim (two cities), Sibmah (fragrance), Zereth-shahar (splendor of the dawn) on the hill in the valley,
13.20: Beth-peor (house of Peor), the slopes of Pisgah (cleft), and Beth-jeshimoth (house of the desolation)—
13.21: all the cities of the plateau and all the kingdom of Sihon (warrior) king of the Amorites (sayers), who reigned in Heshbon (stronghold) until Moses (drawn out) killed him and the chiefs of Midian (strife) (Evi (my desire), Rekem (variegation), Zur (rock), Hur (hole), and Reba (four)), the princes of Sihon who lived in the land.
13.22: The Israelites (he wrestles with God) also killed the diviner Balaam (not of the people) son of Beor (burning) along with the others they put to the sword.
13.23: And the border of the Reubenites (behold a son) was the bank of the Jordan (flowing down). This was the inheritance of the clans of the Reubenites, including the cities and villages.
13.24: This is what Moses (drawn out) had given to the clans of the tribe of Gad (troop):
13.25: The territory of Jazer (helped), all the cities of Gilead (rocky region), and half the land of the Ammonites (tribal) as far as Aroer (ruins), near Rabbah (great);
13.26: the territory from Heshbon (stronghold) to Ramath-mizpeh (high place of the watchtower) and Betonim (pistachio nuts), and from Mahanaim (two camps) to the border of Debir [Li-debir];
13.27: and in the valley, Beth-haram (place of the height), Beth-nimrah (house of the leopard), Succoth (booths), and Zaphon (north), with the rest of the kingdom of Sihon (warrior) king of Heshbon (stronghold) (flowing down) (the territory on the east side of the Jordan up to the edge of the Sea of Chinnereth (the Sea of Galilee)
13.28: This was the inheritance of the clans of the Gadites, (troop) including the cities and villages.
13.29: This is what Moses (drawn out) had given to the clans of the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget), that is, to half the tribe of the descendants of Manasseh:
13.30: The territory from Mahanaim (two camps) through all Bashan (fruitful)—all the kingdom of Og (long-necked) king of Bashan, including all the towns of Jair (he enlightens) that are in Bashan, sixty cities;
13.31: half of Gilead (rocky region); and Ashtaroth (star) and Edrei (good pasture), the royal cities of Og (long-necked) in Bashan (fruitful). All this was for the clans of the descendants of Machir (sold) son of Manasseh (causing to forget), that is, half of the descendants of Machir.
13.32: These were the portions Moses (drawn out) had given them on the plains of Moab (of his father) beyond the Jordan (flowing down), east of Jericho (city of palms).
13.33: To the tribe of Levi (joined to), however (drawn out), Moses had given no inheritance. The LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), is their inheritance, just as He had promised them.

14.1: Now these are the portions that the Israelites (God prevails) inherited in the land of Canaan (lowland), as distributed by Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), and the heads of the families of the tribes of Israel.
14.2: Their inheritance was assigned by lot for the nine and a half tribes, as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded through Moses (drawn out).
14.3: For Moses (drawn out) had given the inheritance east of the Jordan (flowing down) to the other two and a half tribes. But he granted no inheritance among them to the Levites.
14.4: The descendants of Joseph (YHWH has added) became two tribes, Manasseh (causing to forget) and Ephraim (doubly fruitful). And no portion of the land was given to the Levites, except for cities in which to live, along with pasturelands for their flocks and herds.
14.5: So the Israelites (God prevails) did as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded Moses (drawn out), and they divided the land.
14.6: Then the sons of Judah (praised) approached Joshua (YHWH saves) at Gilgal (a wheel), and Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) the Kenizzite said to him, “You know what the LORD {YHWH} said to Moses (drawn out) the man of God {Elohim} at Kadesh-barnea (holy) about you and me.
14.7: I was forty years old when Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD {YHWH} sent me from Kadesh-barnea (holy) to spy out the land, and I brought back to him an honest report.
14.8: Although my brothers who went with me made the hearts of the people melt with fear, I remained loyal to the LORD my God {YHWH Elohay}.
14.9: On that day Moses (drawn out) swore to me, saying, ‘Surely the land on which you have set foot will be an inheritance to you and your children forever, because you have wholly followed the LORD my God {YHWH Elohay}.
14.10: Now behold, as the LORD promised, He {YHWH} has kept me alive these forty-five years since He spoke this word to Moses (drawn out), while Israel (he wrestles with God) wandered in the wilderness. So here I am today, eighty-five years old,
14.11: still as strong today as I was the day Moses (drawn out) sent me out. As my strength was then, so it is now for war, for going out, and for coming in.
14.12: Now therefore give me this hill country that the LORD {YHWH} promised me on that day, for you yourself heard then that the Anakim (long-necked) were there, with great and fortified cities. Perhaps with the LORD’s help I will drive them out, as the LORD has spoken.
14.13: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) blessed Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) and gave him Hebron (association) as his inheritance.
14.14: Therefore Hebron (association) belongs to Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) the Kenizzite as an inheritance to this day, because he wholly followed the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God).
14.15: (Hebron (association) used to be called Kiriath-arba (city of Arba), after Arba, the greatest man among the Anakim (long-necked). Then the land had rest from war.

15.1: Now the allotment for the clans of the tribe of Judah (praised) extended to the border of Edom (red), to the Wilderness of Zin (flat) at the extreme southern boundary:
15.2: Their southern border started at the bay on the southern tip of the Salt Sea (the Dead Sea),
15.3: proceeded south of the Ascent (ascent of scorpions) of Akrabbim (the Ascent of Scorpions), continued on to Zin (flat), went over to the south of Kadesh-barnea (holy), ran past Hezron (surrounded by a wall) up to Addar (exceedingly glorious), and curved toward Karka (floor).
15.4: It proceeded to Azmon (strong), joined the Brook of Egypt (land of bondage), and ended at the Sea (the Mediterranean Sea). This was their [your] southern border.
15.5: The eastern border was the Salt Sea as far as the mouth of the Jordan (flowing down). The northern border started from the bay of the sea at the mouth of the Jordan,
15.6: went up to Beth-hoglah (house of the partridge), proceeded north of Beth-arabah (house of the desert valley), and went up to the Stone of Bohan (thumb) son of Reuben (behold a son).
15.7: Then the border went up to Debir (sanctuary) from the Valley of Achor, turning north to Gilgal (a wheel), which faces the Ascent of Adummim (ruddy one) south of the ravine. It continued along the waters of En-shemesh (fountain of the sun) and came out at En-rogel (fount of the fuller).
15.8: From there the border went up the Valley of Ben-hinnom (lamentation) along the southern slope of the Jebusites (Jerusalemites) (that is, Jerusalem (city of peace)) and ascended to the top of the hill that faces the Valley of Hinnom on the west, at the northern end of the Valley of Rephaim.
15.9: From the hilltop the border curved to the spring of the Waters of Nephtoah (opening), proceeded to the cities of Mount Ephron (fawn-like), and then bent around toward Baalah (mistress) (that is, Kiriath-jearim (city of forests)).
15.10: The border curled westward from Baalah (mistress) to Mount Seir (shaggy), ran along the northern slope of Mount Jearim (forests) (that is, Chesalon (hopes)), went down to Beth-shemesh (house of the sun), and crossed to Timnah (portion).
15.11: Then it went out to the northern slope of Ekron (torn up by the roots), curved toward Shikkeron (drunkenness), proceeded to Mount Baalah (mistress), went on to Jabneel (God causes to build), and ended at the Sea.
15.12: And the western border was the coastline of the Great Sea. These are the boundaries around the clans of the descendants of Judah (praised).
15.13: According to the LORD’s {YHWH} command to him (YHWH saves), Joshua gave Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) a portion among the sons of Judah (praised)—Kiriath-arba (city of Arba), that is, Hebron (association). (Arba was the forefather of Anak (neck).)
15.14: And Caleb (dog) drove out from there the three sons of Anak (neck)—the descendants of Sheshai (noble), Ahiman (my brother is a gift), and Talmai (furrowed), the children of Anak.
15.15: From there he marched against the inhabitants of Debir (sanctuary) (formerly known as Kiriath-sepher (city of the book)).
15.16: And Caleb (dog) said, “To the man who strikes down Kiriath-sepher (city of the book) and captures it, I will give my daughter Acsah (anklet) in marriage.
15.17: So Othniel (lion of God) son of Caleb’s (dog) brother Kenaz (hunter) captured the city, and Caleb gave his daughter Acsah (anklet) to him in marriage.
15.18: One day Acsah came to Othniel and urged him [and he urged her] to ask her father for a field. When she got off her donkey, Caleb (dog) asked her, “What do you desire?
15.19: “Give me a blessing, she answered. “Since you have given me land in the Negev (southland), give me springs of water as well. So Caleb gave her both the upper and lower springs.
15.20: This is the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Judah (praised).
15.21: These were the southernmost cities of the tribe of Judah (praised) in the Negev (southland) toward the border of Edom (red): Kabzeel (God gathers), Eder (a flock), Jagur (he sojourns),
15.22: Kinah (lamentation), Dimonah (river bed / silence), Adadah (festival / boundary),
15.25: Hazor-hadattah (new Hazor), Kerioth-hezron (cities) (surrounded by a wall) (that is, Hazor (castle)),
15.27: Hazar-gaddah (village of good fortune), Heshmon (rich soil), Beth-pelet (house of escape),
15.28: Hazar-shual (jackal village), Beersheba (well of seven), Biziothiah (contempt of YHWH),
15.32: Lebaoth (witness), Shilhim (fountains), Ain (spring), and Rimmon (pomegranate)—twenty-nine cities in all, along with their villages.
15.33: These were in the foothills {Shephelah}: Eshtaol (entreaty), Zorah (hornet), Ashnah (I will cause change),
15.34: Zanoah (cast off), En-gannim (fountain of the garden), Tappuah (the apple city), Enam (double spring),
15.35: Jarmuth (heights), Adullam (justice of the people), Socoh (bushy), Azekah (dug over),
15.36: Shaaraim (double gate), Adithaim (double ornament), and Gederah (wall) (or Gederothaim (two walls))—fourteen cities, along with their villages.
15.38: Dilan (gourd), Mizpeh (watchtower), Joktheel (the blessedness of God),
15.41: Gederoth (walls), Beth-dagon (house of Dagon), Naamah (loveliness), and Makkedah (place of shepherds)—sixteen cities, along with their villages.
15.43: Iphtah (he opens), Ashnah (I will cause change), Nezib (garrison),
15.44: Keilah (fortress), Achzib (deception), and Mareshah (crest of a hill)—nine cities, along with their villages.
15.45: Ekron (torn up by the roots), with its towns and villages;
15.46: from Ekron (torn up by the roots) to the sea, all the cities near Ashdod (powerful), along with their villages;
15.47: Ashdod (powerful), with its towns and villages; Gaza (the strong), with its towns and villages, as far as the Brook of Egypt (land of bondage) and the coastline of the Great Sea.
15.48: These were in the hill country: Shamir (a point), Jattir (plenty), Socoh (bushy),
15.49: Dannah (You have judged), Kiriath-sannah (city of the book) (that is, Debir (sanctuary)),
15.51: Goshen (drawing near), Holon (sandy), and Giloh (exile)—eleven cities, along with their villages.
15.53: Janim (asleep), Beth-tappuah (house of the apple), Aphekah (enclosure),
15.54: Humtah (place of lizards), Kiriath-arba (city of Arba) (that is, Hebron (association)), and Zior (smallness)—nine cities, along with their villages.
15.56: Jezreel (God sows), Jokdeam (burning of a people), Zanoah (cast off),
15.57: Kain (possession), Gibeah (hill), and Timnah (portion)—ten cities, along with their villages.
15.58: Halhul (trembling), Beth-zur (house of the rock), Gedor (wall),
15.59: Maarath (bareness), Beth-anoth (house of afflictions), and Eltekon (God is straight)—six cities, along with their villages.
15.60: Kiriath-baal (city of Baal) (that is, Kiriath-jearim (city of forests)), and Rabbah (great)—two cities, along with their villages.
15.61: These were in the wilderness: Beth-arabah (house of the desert valley), Middin (measures), Secacah (thicket),
15.62: Nibshan (soft soil), the City of Salt (the city of salt), and En-gedi (fount of the kid)—six cities, along with their villages.
15.63: But the descendants of Judah (praised) could not drive out the Jebusites (Jerusalemites) living in Jerusalem (city of peace). So to this day the Jebusites live there among the descendants of Judah.

16.1: The allotment for the descendants of Joseph (YHWH has added) extended from the Jordan (flowing down) at Jericho (city of palms) to the waters of Jericho on the east, through the wilderness that goes up from Jericho into the hill country of Bethel (house of God).
16.2: It went on from Bethel (house of God) (that is, Luz [from Bethel to Luz]) and proceeded to the border of the Archites (lengthy) in Ataroth (crowns).
16.3: Then it descended westward to the border of the Japhletites (let him escape) as far as the border of (house of hollowness) Lower Beth-horon and on to Gezer (portion), and it ended at the Sea (the Mediterranean Sea).
16.4: So Ephraim (doubly fruitful) and Manasseh (causing to forget), the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added), received their inheritance.
16.5: This was the territory of the descendants of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) by their clans: The border of their inheritance went from Ataroth-addar (crowns of glory) in the east to Upper Beth-horon (house of hollowness)
16.6: and out toward the Sea. From Michmethath (hiding place) on the north it turned eastward toward Taanath-shiloh (approach to Shiloh) and passed by it to Janoah (He rests) on the east.
16.7: From Janoah (He rests) it went down to Ataroth (crowns) and Naarah (maiden), and then reached Jericho (city of palms) and came out at the Jordan (flowing down).
16.8: From Tappuah (the apple city) the border went westward to the Brook of Kanah (reed) and ended at the Sea. This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful),
16.9: along with all the cities and villages set apart for the descendants of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) within the inheritance of Manasseh (causing to forget).
16.10: But they did not drive out the Canaanites (merchants) who lived in Gezer (portion). So the Canaanites dwell among the Ephraimites (doubly fruitful) to this day, but they are forced laborers.

17.1: Now this was the allotment for the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) as Joseph’s (YHWH has added) firstborn son, namely for Machir (sold) the firstborn of Manasseh and father of the Gileadites (rocky region), who had received Gilead and Bashan (fruitful) because Machir was a man of war.
17.2: So this allotment was for the rest of the descendants of Manasseh (causing to forget)—the clans of Abiezer (Iezer), Helek (portion), Asriel (I shall be prince of God), Shechem (shoulder), Hepher (a well), and Shemida (wise). These are the other male descendants of the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph (YHWH has added).
17.3: But Zelophehad (first-born) son of Hepher (a well) (the son of Gilead (rocky region), the son of Machir (sold), the son of Manasseh (causing to forget)) had no sons but only daughters. These are the names of his daughters: Mahlah (disease), Noah (motion), Hoglah (partridge), Milcah (queen), and Tirzah (favorable).
17.4: They approached Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), and the leaders, and said, “The LORD {YHWH} commanded Moses (drawn out) to give us an inheritance among our brothers. So Joshua gave them an inheritance among their father’s brothers, in keeping with the command of the LORD.
17.5: Thus ten shares fell to Manasseh (causing to forget), in addition to the land of Gilead (rocky region) and Bashan (fruitful) beyond the Jordan (flowing down),
17.6: because the daughters of Manasseh (causing to forget) received an inheritance among his sons. And the land of Gilead (rocky region) belonged to the rest of the sons of Manasseh.
17.7: Now the border of Manasseh (causing to forget) went from Asher (happy) to Michmethath (hiding place) near Shechem (shoulder), then southward to include the inhabitants of En-tappuah (fountain of the apple-city).
17.8: The region of Tappuah (the apple city) belonged to Manasseh (causing to forget), but Tappuah itself, on the border of Manasseh, belonged to Ephraim (doubly fruitful).
17.9: From there the border continued southward to the Brook of Kanah (reed). There were cities belonging to Ephraim (doubly fruitful) among the cities of Manasseh (causing to forget), but the border of Manasseh was on the north side of the brook and ended at the Sea (the Mediterranean Sea).
17.10: Ephraim’s territory (doubly fruitful) was to the south, and Manasseh’s (causing to forget) was to the north, having the Sea as its border and adjoining Asher (happy) on the north and Issachar (there is recompense) on the east.
17.11: Within Issachar (there is recompense) and Asher (happy) (house of ease), Manasseh (causing to forget) was assigned Beth-shean, Ibleam (devouring the people), Dor (generation) (that is, Naphath) Endor (fountain of Dor), Taanach (sandy), and Megiddo (place of crowds), each with their surrounding settlements.
17.12: But the descendants of Manasseh (causing to forget) were unable to occupy these cities, because the Canaanites (merchants) were determined to stay in this land.
17.13: However, when the Israelites (God prevails) grew stronger, they put the Canaanites (merchants) to forced labor; but they failed to drive them out completely.
17.14: Then the sons of Joseph (YHWH has added) said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “Why have you given us only one portion as an inheritance? We have many people, because the LORD {YHWH} has blessed us abundantly.
17.15: Joshua (YHWH saves) answered them, “If you have so many people that the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) is too small for you, go to the forest and clear for yourself an area in the land of the Perizzites (villagers) and the Rephaim.
17.16: “The hill country is not enough for us, they (YHWH has added) replied, “and all the Canaanites (merchants) who live in the valley (house of ease) have iron chariots, both in Beth-shean with its towns and in the Valley of Jezreel (God sows).
17.17: So Joshua (YHWH saves) said to the house of Joseph (YHWH has added)—to Ephraim (doubly fruitful) and Manasseh (causing to forget)—“You have many people and great strength. You shall not have just one allotment,
17.18: because the hill country will be yours as well. It is a forest; clear it, and its farthest limits will be yours. Although the Canaanites have iron chariots and although they are strong, you can drive them out (merchants).

18.1: Then the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God) assembled at Shiloh (place of rest) and set up the Tent of Meeting there. And though the land was subdued before them,
18.2: there were still seven tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God) who had not yet received their inheritance.
18.3: So Joshua (YHWH saves) said to the Israelites (God prevails), “How long will you? put off entering and possessing the land that the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of your? fathers, has given you??
18.4: Appoint three men from each tribe, and I will send them out to survey the land and map it out, according to the inheritance of each. Then they will return to me
18.5: and divide the land into seven portions. Judah (praised) shall remain in their territory in the south, and the house of Joseph (YHWH has added) shall remain in their territory in the north.
18.6: When you? have mapped out the seven portions of land and brought it to me, I will cast lots for you? here in the presence of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}.
18.7: The Levites, however, have no portion among you?, because their inheritance is the priesthood of the LORD {YHWH}. And Gad (troop), Reuben (behold a son), and half the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) have already received the inheritance that Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD gave them beyond the Jordan (flowing down) to the east.
18.8: As the men got up to go out, Joshua (YHWH saves) commanded them to map out the land, saying, “Go and survey the land, map it out, and return to me. Then I will cast lots for you? here in Shiloh (place of rest) in the presence of the LORD {YHWH}.
18.9: So the men departed and went throughout the land, mapping it city by city into seven portions. Then they returned with the document to Joshua (YHWH saves) at the camp in Shiloh (place of rest).
18.10: And Joshua (YHWH saves) cast lots for them in the presence of the LORD {YHWH} at Shiloh (place of rest), where he distributed the land to the Israelites (God prevails) according to their divisions.
18.11: The first lot came up for the clans of the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand). Their allotted territory lay between the tribes of Judah (praised) and Joseph (YHWH has added):
18.12: On the north side their border began at the Jordan (flowing down), went up past the northern slope of Jericho (city of palms), headed west through the hill country, and came out at the wilderness of Beth-aven (house of wickedness).
18.13: From there the border crossed over (almond tree) to the southern slope of Luz (that is, Bethel (house of God)) and went down to Ataroth-addar (crowns of glory) on the hill south of (house of hollowness) Lower Beth-horon.
18.14: On the west side the border curved southward from the hill facing Beth-horon (house of hollowness) on the south and came out at Kiriath-baal (city of Baal) (that is, Kiriath-jearim (city of forests)), a city of the sons of Judah (praised). This was the western side.
18.15: On the south side the border began at the outskirts of Kiriath-jearim (city of forests) and extended westward to the spring at the Waters of Nephtoah (opening).
18.16: Then it went down to the foot of the hill that faces the Valley of Ben-hinnom (lamentation) at the northern end of the Valley of Rephaim and ran down the Valley of Hinnom toward the southern slope of the Jebusites (Jerusalemites) and downward to En-rogel (fount of the fuller).
18.17: From there it curved northward and proceeded to En-shemesh (fountain of the sun) and on to Geliloth (circuits) facing the Ascent of Adummim (ruddy one), and continued down to the Stone of Bohan (thumb) son of Reuben (behold a son).
18.18: Then it went on to the northern slope of Beth-arabah [slope facing the Arabah] and went down into the valley.
18.19: The border continued to the northern slope of Beth-hoglah (house of the partridge) and came out at the northern bay of the Salt Sea (the Dead Sea), at the mouth of the Jordan (flowing down). This was the southern border.
18.20: On the east side the border was the Jordan (flowing down). These were the borders around the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand).
18.21: These were the cities of the clans of the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand): Jericho (city of palms), Beth-hoglah (house of the partridge), Emek-keziz (cut off),
18.22: Beth-arabah (house of the desert valley), Zemaraim (double fleece of wool), Bethel (house of God),
18.24: Chephar-ammoni (village of the Ammonites), Ophni (mouldy), and Geba (hill)—twelve cities, along with their villages.
18.28: Zelah (a rib), Haeleph (thousand), Jebus [the Jebusite] (that is, Jerusalem (city of peace)), Gibeah \Gibeath/, and Kiriath-jearim [Kiriath] fourteen cities, along with their villages. This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Benjamin (son of the right hand).

19.1: The second lot came out for the clans of the tribe of Simeon (heard): Their inheritance lay within the territory of Judah (praised)
19.2: and included Beersheba (well of seven) (or Sheba (seven)), Moladah (birth / race),
19.4: Eltolad (God's generations), Bethul (dweller in God), Hormah (destruction),
19.5: Ziklag (winding), Beth-marcaboth (house of the chariots), Hazar-susah (horse village),
19.6: Beth-lebaoth (house of lionesses), and Sharuhen (refuge of grace)—thirteen cities, along with their villages.
19.7: Ain (spring), Rimmon (pomegranate), Ether (abundant), and Ashan (smoke)—four cities, along with their villages,
19.8: and all the villages surrounding these cities as far as Baalath-beer (mistress of the well) (Ramah (hill) of the Negev (southland)) This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Simeon (heard).
19.9: The inheritance of the Simeonites (heard) was taken from the territory of Judah (praised), because the share for Judah’s descendants was too large for them. So the Simeonites received an inheritance within Judah’s portion.
19.10: The third lot came up for the clans of the tribe of Zebulun (exalted): The border of their inheritance stretched as far as Sarid (survivor).
19.11: It went up westward to Maralah (trembling), reached Dabbesheth (a hill-place), and met the brook east of Jokneam (the people lament).
19.12: From Sarid (survivor) it turned eastward along the border of Chisloth-tabor (flanks of Tabor) and went on to Daberath (word) and up to Japhia (shining).
19.13: From there it crossed eastward to Gath-hepher (the winepress of digging) and to Eth-kazin (time of the judge); it extended to Rimmon (pomegranate) and curved around toward Neah (shaking).
19.14: Then the border circled around the north side of Neah to Hannathon (gracious) and ended at the Valley of Iphtah-el (God opens).
19.15: It also included Kattath (small), Nahalal (pasture), Shimron (watch-height), Idalah (memorial of God), and Bethlehem (house of bread). There were twelve cities, along with their villages.
19.16: This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Zebulun (exalted), including these cities and their villages.
19.17: The fourth lot came out for the clans (there is recompense) of the tribe of Issachar:
19.18: Their territory included Jezreel (God sows), Chesulloth (flanks), Shunem (double resting place),
19.19: Hapharaim (two pits), Shion (ruin), Anaharath (the groaning of fear),
19.20: Rabbith (multitude), Kishion (hard), Ebez (I will make white),
19.21: Remeth (height), En-gannim (fountain of the garden), En-haddah (swift fountain), and Beth-pazzez (house of dispersion).
19.22: The border reached Tabor (mound), Shahazumah (toward the heights), and Beth-shemesh (house of the sun), and ended at the Jordan (flowing down). There were sixteen cities, along with their villages.
19.23: This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Issachar (there is recompense), including these cities and their villages.
19.24: The fifth lot came out for the clans of the tribe of Asher (happy):
19.25: Their territory included Helkath (smooth), Hali (necklace), Beten (womb), Achshaph (I shall be bewitched),
19.26: Allammelech (oak of the king), Amad (enduring), and Mishal (entreaty). On the west the border touched Carmel (garden-land) and Shihor-libnath (blackness of whiteness),
19.27: then turned eastward toward Beth-dagon (house of Dagon), touched Zebulun (exalted) and the Valley of Iphtah-el (God opens), and went north to Beth-emek (house of the valley) and Neiel (moved by God), passing Cabul (binding) on the left.
19.28: It went on to Ebron [Abdon], Rehob (broad place), Hammon (warm springs), and Kanah (reed), as far as Greater Sidon (hunting).
19.29: The border then turned back toward Ramah (hill) as far as the fortified city of Tyre (a rock), turned toward Hosah (refuge), and came out at the Sea (the Mediterranean Sea) in the region of Achzib (deception),
19.30: Ummah (union), Aphek (enclosure), and Rehob (broad place). There were twenty-two cities, along with their villages.
19.31: This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Asher (happy), including these cities and their villages.
19.32: The sixth lot came out for the clans of the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling):
19.33: Their border started at Heleph (exchange) and the great tree (oak) of Zaanannim (removings), passing Adami-nekeb (man of Adar) and Jabneel (God causes to build) as far as Lakkum (fortification) and ending at the Jordan (flowing down).
19.34: Then the border turned westward to Aznoth-tabor (peaks of Tabor) and ran from there to Hukkok (appointed), touching Zebulun (exalted) on the south side, Asher (happy) on the west, and Judah (praised) at the Jordan [west, and the Jordan] on the east.
19.35: The fortified cities were Ziddim (the declivities), Zer (flint), Hammath (hot spring), Rakkath (shore), Chinnereth (harps),
19.37: Kedesh (holy place), Edrei (good pasture), En-hazor (fount of Hazor),
19.38: Iron (fearing), Migdal-el (tower of God), Horem (sacred), Beth-anath (house of affliction), and Beth-shemesh (house of the sun). There were nineteen cities, along with their villages.
19.39: This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Naphtali (wrestling), including these cities and their villages.
19.40: The seventh lot came out for the clans of the tribe of Dan (He has judged):
19.41: The territory of their inheritance included Zorah (hornet), Eshtaol (entreaty), Ir-shemesh (city of the sun-god),
19.42: Shaalabbin (place of foxes), Aijalon (field of deer), Ithlah (he hangs),
19.43: Elon (mighty), Timnah (portion), Ekron (torn up by the roots),
19.44: Eltekeh (let God spew you out), Gibbethon (mound), Baalath (mistress),
19.45: Jehud (beauty), Bene-berak (sons of lightning), Gath-rimmon (winepress of the pomegranate),
19.46: Me-jarkon (waters of yellowness), and Rakkon (the temple), including the territory across from Joppa (beautiful).
19.47: (Later, when the territory of the Danites (He has judged) was lost to them, they went up and fought against Leshem (precious stone), captured it, and put it to the sword. So they took possession of Leshem, settled there, and renamed it after their father Dan.
19.48: This was the inheritance of the clans of the tribe of Dan (He has judged), including these cities and their villages.
19.49: When they had finished distributing the land into its territories, the Israelites (God prevails) gave Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity) an inheritance among them,
19.50: as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded. They gave him the city of Timnath-serah (Timnath-heres) in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), as he requested. He rebuilt the city and settled in it.
19.51: These are the inheritances that Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), and the heads of the families distributed by lot to the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God) at Shiloh (place of rest) before the LORD {YHWH} at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. So they finished dividing up the land.

20.1: Then the LORD {YHWH} said to Joshua (YHWH saves),
20.2: “Tell the Israelites (God prevails) to designate the cities of refuge, as I instructed you? through Moses (drawn out),
20.3: so that anyone who kills another unintentionally or accidentally may flee there. These will be your? refuge from the avenger of blood.
20.4: When someone flees to one of these cities, stands at the entrance of the city gate, and states his case before its elders, they are to bring him into the city and give him a place to live among them.
20.5: Now if the avenger of blood pursues him, they must not surrender the manslayer into his hand, because that man killed his neighbor accidentally without prior malice.
20.6: He is to stay in that city until he stands trial before the assembly and until the death of the high priest serving at that time. Then the manslayer may return to his own home in the city from which he fled.
20.7: So they set apart Kedesh (holy place) in Galilee (circuit) in the hill country of Naphtali (wrestling), Shechem (shoulder) in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), and Kiriath-arba (city of Arba) (that is, Hebron (association)) in the hill country of Judah (praised).
20.8: And beyond the Jordan (flowing down), east of Jericho (city of palms), they designated Bezer (gold ore) on the wilderness plateau from the tribe of Reuben (behold a son), Ramoth (heights) in Gilead (rocky region) from the tribe of Gad (troop), and Golan (their captivity) in Bashan (fruitful) from the tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget).
20.9: These are the cities appointed for all the Israelites (God prevails) and foreigners among them, so that anyone who kills a person unintentionally may flee there and not die by the hand of the avenger of blood prior to standing trial before the assembly.

21.1: Now the family heads of the Levites approached Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), and the heads of the other tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God)
21.2: at Shiloh (place of rest) in the land of Canaan (lowland) and said to them, “The LORD {YHWH} commanded through Moses (drawn out) that we be given cities in which to live, together with pasturelands for our livestock.
21.3: So by the command of the LORD {YHWH}, the Israelites (God prevails) gave the Levites these cities and their pasturelands out of their own inheritance:
21.4: The first lot came out for the Kohathite clans. The Levites who were descendants of Aaron (light bringer) the priest received thirteen cities by lot from the tribes of Judah (praised), Simeon, and Benjamin (son of the right hand).
21.5: The remaining descendants of Kohath (assembly) received ten cities by lot from the tribes of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), Dan (He has judged), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget).
21.6: The descendants of Gershon (exile) received thirteen cities by lot from the tribes of Issachar (there is recompense), Asher (happy), Naphtali (wrestling), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) in Bashan (fruitful).
21.7: And the descendants of Merari (bitter) received twelve cities from the tribes of Reuben (behold a son), Gad (troop), and Zebulun (exalted).
21.8: So the Israelites (God prevails) allotted to the Levites these cities, together with their pasturelands, as the LORD {YHWH} had commanded through Moses (drawn out).
21.9: From the tribes of Judah (praised) and Simeon (heard), they designated these cities by name
21.10: to the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) from the Kohathite clans of the Levites (joined to), because the first lot fell to them:
21.11: They gave them Kiriath-arba (city of Arba) (that is, Hebron (association)), with its surrounding pasturelands, in the hill country of Judah (praised). (Arba was the father of Anak (neck).
21.12: But they had given the fields and villages around the city to Caleb (dog) son of Jephunneh (He will be facing) as his possession.
21.13: So to the descendants of Aaron (light bringer) the priest they gave these cities, together with their pasturelands: Hebron (association), a city of refuge for the manslayer, Libnah (pavement),
21.16: Ain (spring), Juttah (stretched out), and Beth-shemesh (house of the sun)—nine cities from these two tribes, together with their pasturelands.
21.17: And from the tribe of Benjamin they gave (son of the right hand) them Gibeon (hill city), Geba (hill),
21.18: Anathoth (answers to prayer), and Almon (concealed)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.19: In all, thirteen cities, together with their pasturelands, were given to the priests, the descendants of Aaron (light bringer).
21.20: The remaining Kohathite (assembly) clans of the Levites were allotted these cities: From the tribe of Ephraim (doubly fruitful)
21.21: they were given Shechem (shoulder) in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) (a city of refuge for the manslayer), Gezer (portion),
21.22: Kibzaim (two gatherings), and Beth-horon (house of hollowness)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.23: From the tribe of Dan they were given (He has judged) Elteke (let God spew you out), Gibbethon (mound),
21.24: Aijalon (field of deer), and Gath-rimmon (winepress of the pomegranate)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.25: And from the half-tribe of Manasseh they were given (causing to forget) Taanach (sandy) and Gath-rimmon (winepress of the pomegranate)—two cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.26: In all, ten cities, together with their pasturelands, were given to the rest of the Kohathite (assembly) clans.
21.27: This is what the Levite clans of the Gershonites were given (exile): From the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) they were given Golan (their captivity) in Bashan (fruitful), a city of refuge for the manslayer, and Beeshterah (with increase)—two cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.28: From the tribe of Issachar they were given (there is recompense) Kishion (hard), Daberath (word),
21.29: Jarmuth (heights), and En-gannim (fountain of the garden)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.30: From the tribe of Asher they were given (happy) Mishal (entreaty), Abdon (servile),
21.31: Helkath (smooth), and Rehob (broad place)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.32: And from the tribe of Naphtali they were given (wrestling) Kedesh (holy place) in Galilee (circuit) (a city of refuge for the manslayer) Hammoth-dor (hot springs of Dor), and Kartan (two cities)—three cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.33: In all, thirteen cities, together with their pasturelands, were given to the Gershonite clans.
21.34: This is what the Merarite (bitter) clan (the rest of the Levites) were given: From the tribe of Zebulun they were given (exalted) Jokneam (the people lament), Kartah (city),
21.35: Dimnah (dunghill), and Nahalal (pasture)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.36: From the tribe of Reuben they were given (behold a son) Bezer (gold ore), Jahaz (trodden down),
21.37: Kedemoth (eastern), and Mephaath (splendor / height)—four cities, together with their pasturelands.
21.38: And from the tribe of Gad they were given (troop) Ramoth (heights) in Gilead (rocky region), a city of refuge for the manslayer, Mahanaim (two camps),
21.39: Heshbon (stronghold), and Jazer (helped)—four cities in all, together with their pasturelands.
21.40: In all, twelve cities were allotted to the clans of Merari (bitter), the remaining Levite clans.
21.41: For the Levites, then, there were forty-eight cities in all, together with their pasturelands, within the territory of the Israelites (God prevails).
21.42: Each of these cities had its own surrounding pasturelands; this was true for all the cities.
21.43: Thus the LORD {YHWH} gave Israel (he wrestles with God) all the land He had sworn to give their fathers, and they took possession of it and settled in it.
21.44: And the LORD {YHWH} gave them rest on every side, just as He had sworn to their fathers. None of their enemies could stand against them, for the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand.
21.45: Not one of all the LORD’s {YHWH} good promises to the house of Israel (he wrestles with God) had failed; everything was fulfilled.

22.1: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) summoned the Reubenites, the Gadites (a troop / fortune), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget)
22.2: and told them, “You? have done all that Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD {YHWH} commanded you?, and you? have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you?.
22.3: All this time you? have not deserted your? brothers, up to this very day, but have kept the charge given you? by the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}.
22.4: And now that the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} has given your? brothers rest as He promised them, you? may return to your? homes in the land that Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD gave you? across the Jordan (flowing down).
22.5: But be very careful to observe the commandment and the law that Moses (drawn out) the servant of the LORD {YHWH} gave you?: to love the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}, to walk in all His ways, to keep His commandments, to hold fast to Him, and to serve Him with all your? heart and with all your? soul.
22.6: So Joshua (YHWH saves) blessed them and sent them on their way, and they went to their homes.
22.7: (To the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) Moses (drawn out) had given land in Bashan (fruitful), and to the other half Joshua (YHWH saves) gave land on the west side of the Jordan (flowing down) among their brothers. When Joshua sent them to their homes he blessed them,
22.8: saying, “Return to your? homes with your great wealth, with immense herds of livestock, with silver, gold, bronze, iron, and very many clothes. Divide with your? brothers the spoil of your? enemies.
22.9: So the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) left the Israelites (God prevails) at Shiloh (place of rest) in the land of Canaan (lowland) to return to their own land of Gilead (rocky region), which they had acquired according to the command of the LORD {YHWH} through Moses (drawn out).
22.10: And when they came to Geliloth (to the circle of stones) near the Jordan (flowing down) in the land of Canaan (lowland), the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) built an imposing altar there by the Jordan.
22.11: Then the Israelites (God prevails) received the report: “Behold, the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) have built an altar on the border of the land of Canaan (lowland), at Geliloth (circuit) near the Jordan (flowing down) on the Israelite side.
22.12: And when they (he wrestles with God) heard this, the whole congregation of Israel assembled at Shiloh (place of rest) to go to war against them.
22.13: The Israelites (God prevails) sent Phinehas (mouth of brass) son of Eleazar (God has helped) the priest to the land of Gilead (rocky region), to the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget).
22.14: With him they sent ten chiefs—one family leader from each tribe of Israel (he wrestles with God), each the head of a family among the clans of Israel.
22.15: They went to the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) in the land of Gilead (rocky region) and said to them,
22.16: “This is what the whole congregation of the LORD {YHWH} says: ‘What is this breach of faith you? have committed today against the God {Elohim} of Israel (he wrestles with God) by turning away from the LORD and building for yourselves an altar, that you? might rebel against the LORD this day?
22.17: Was not the sin of Peor (cleft) enough for us, from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day? It even brought a plague upon the congregation of the LORD {YHWH}.
22.18: And now, would you? turn away from the LORD {YHWH}? If you? rebel today against the LORD, tomorrow He will be angry with the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God).
22.19: If indeed the land of your? inheritance is unclean, then cross over to the land of the LORD’s {YHWH} possession, where the LORD’s tabernacle stands, and take possession of it among us. But do not rebel against the LORD or against us by building for yourselves an altar other than the altar of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}.
22.20: Was not Achan (troubler) son of Zerah (rising) unfaithful regarding what was set apart for destruction {cherem}, bringing wrath upon the whole congregation of Israel (he wrestles with God)? Yet it was not only Achan who perished because of his sin!
22.21: Then the Reubenites (behold a son), the Gadites (troop), and the half-tribe of Manasseh (causing to forget) answered the leaders of the clans of Israel (he wrestles with God):
22.22: “The LORD {YHWH}, the Mighty One, is God {Elohim}! The LORD, the Mighty One, is God! He knows, and may Israel (he wrestles with God) also know. If this was in rebellion or breach of faith against the LORD, do not spare us today.
22.23: If we have built for ourselves an altar to turn away from Him {YHWH} and to offer burnt offerings and grain offerings on it, or to sacrifice peace offerings on it, may the LORD Himself hold us accountable.
22.24: But in fact we have done this for fear that in the future your? descendants might say to ours, ‘What have you? to do with the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God)?
22.25: For the LORD {YHWH} has made the Jordan (flowing down) a border between us and you? Reubenites (behold a son) and Gadites (troop). You? have no share in the LORD! So your? descendants could cause ours to stop fearing the LORD.
22.26: That is why we said, ‘Let us take action and build an altar for ourselves, but not for burnt offerings or sacrifices.
22.27: Rather, let it be a witness between us and you? and the generations to come, that we will worship the LORD {YHWH} in His presence with our burnt offerings, sacrifices, and peace offerings. Then in the future, your? descendants cannot say to ours, ‘You? have no share in the LORD!
22.28: Therefore we said, ‘If they ever say this to us or to our descendants, we will answer: Look at the replica of the altar of the LORD {YHWH} that our fathers made, not for burnt offerings or sacrifices, but as a witness between us and you?.
22.29: Far be it from us to rebel against the LORD {YHWH} and turn away from Him today by building an altar for burnt offerings, grain offerings, or sacrifices, other than the altar of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}, which stands before His tabernacle.
22.30: When Phinehas (mouth of brass) the priest and the chiefs of the congregation—the heads of Israel’s (he wrestles with God) clans who were with him—heard what the descendants of Reuben (behold a son), Gad (troop), and Manasseh (causing to forget) had to say, they were satisfied.
22.31: Phinehas (mouth of brass) son of Eleazar (God has helped) the priest said to the descendants of Reuben (behold a son), Gad (troop), and Manasseh (causing to forget), “Today we know that the LORD {YHWH} is among us, because you? have not committed this breach of faith against Him. Consequently, you? have delivered the Israelites (God prevails) from the hand of the LORD.
22.32: Then Phinehas (mouth of brass) son of Eleazar (God has helped) the priest, together with the other leaders, returned to the Israelites (God prevails) in the land of Canaan (lowland) and brought back a report regarding the Reubenites (behold a son) and Gadites (troop) in the land of Gilead (rocky region).
22.33: The Israelites (God prevails) were satisfied with the report, and they blessed God {Elohim} and spoke no more about going to war against them to destroy the land where the Reubenites (behold a son) and Gadites (troop) lived.
22.34: So the Reubenites (behold a son) and Gadites (troop) named the altar Witness, for they said \named the altar, for/, “It is a witness between us that the LORD {YHWH} is God {Elohim}.

23.1: A long time after the LORD {YHWH} had given Israel (he wrestles with God) rest from all the enemies around them, when Joshua (YHWH saves) was old and well along in years,
23.2: he (YHWH saves) summoned all Israel (he wrestles with God), including its elders, leaders, judges, and officers. “I am old and well along in years, he said,
23.3: “and you? have seen everything that the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} has done to all these nations for your? sake, because it was the LORD your? God who fought for you?.
23.4: See, I have allotted as an inheritance to your? tribes these remaining nations, including all the nations I have already cut off, from the Jordan (flowing down) westward to the Great Sea (the Mediterranean Sea).
23.5: The LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} will push them out of your? way and drive them out before you?, so that you? can take possession of their land, as the LORD your? God promised you?.
23.6: Be very strong, then, so that you can keep and obey all that is written in the Book of the Law of Moses (drawn out), not turning aside from it to the right or to the left.
23.7: So you are not to associate with these nations that remain among you?. You? must not call on the names of their gods or swear by them, and you? must not serve them or bow down to them.
23.8: Instead, you? shall hold fast to the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}, as you? have done to this day.
23.9: The LORD {YHWH} has driven out great and powerful nations before you?, and to this day no one can stand against you?.
23.10: One of you? can put a thousand to flight, because the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} fights for you?, just as He promised.
23.11: Therefore watch yourselves carefully, that you love the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}.
23.12: For if you? turn away and cling to the rest of these nations that remain among you?, and if you? intermarry and associate with them,
23.13: know for sure that the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} will no longer drive out these nations before you?. Instead, they will become for you? a snare and a trap, a scourge in your? sides and thorns in your? eyes, until you? perish from this good land that the LORD your? God has given you?.
23.14: Now behold, today I am going the way of all the earth, and you? know with all your? heart and soul that not one of the good promises the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} made to you? has failed. Everything was fulfilled for you?; not one promise has failed.
23.15: But just as every good thing the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} promised you? has come to pass, likewise the LORD will bring upon you? the calamity He has threatened, until He has destroyed you? from this good land He has given you?.
23.16: If you? transgress the covenant of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}, which He commanded you?, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them, then the anger of the LORD will burn against you?, and you? will quickly perish from this good land He has given you?.

24.1: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) assembled all the tribes of Israel (he wrestles with God) at Shechem (shoulder). He summoned the elders, leaders, judges, and officers of Israel, and they presented themselves before God {Elohim}.
24.2: And Joshua (YHWH saves) said to all the people, “This is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘Long ago your? fathers, including Terah (station) the father of Abraham (father of many) and Nahor (snorting), lived beyond the Euphrates \the River/ and worshiped other gods.
24.3: But I took your? father Abraham (father of many) from beyond the Euphrates and led him through all the land of Canaan (lowland), and I multiplied his descendants. I gave him Isaac (he laughs),
24.4: and to Isaac (he laughs) I gave Jacob (he grasps the heel) and Esau (hairy). I gave Esau Mount Seir (shaggy) to possess, but Jacob and his sons went down to Egypt (land of bondage).
24.5: Then I sent Moses (drawn out) and Aaron (light bringer), and I afflicted the Egyptians (land of bondage) by what I did there, and afterward I brought you? out.
24.6: When I brought your? fathers out of Egypt (land of bondage) and you? reached the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds), the Egyptians (double straits) pursued them with chariots and horsemen as far as the Red Sea.
24.7: So your fathers cried out to the LORD {YHWH}, and He put darkness between you? and the Egyptians (double straits), over whom He brought the sea and engulfed them. Your? very eyes saw what I did to the Egyptians (land of bondage). Then you? lived in the wilderness for a long time.
24.8: Later, I brought you? to the land of the Amorites (sayers) who lived beyond the Jordan (flowing down). They fought against you?, but I delivered them into your? hand, that you? should possess their land when I destroyed them before you?.
24.9: Then Balak (devastator) son of Zippor (sparrow), the king of Moab (of his father), set out to fight against Israel (he wrestles with God). He sent for Balaam (not of the people) son of Beor (burning) to curse you?,
24.10: but I would not listen to Balaam (not of the people). So he blessed you? again and again, and I delivered you? from his hand.
24.11: After this, you? crossed the Jordan (flowing down) and came to Jericho (city of palms). The people of Jericho fought against you?, as did the Amorites (sayers), Perizzites (villagers), Canaanites (merchants), Hittites, Girgashites (dwelling on clay), Hivites, and Jebusites (Jerusalemites), and I delivered them into your? hand.
24.12: I sent the hornet ahead of you?, and it drove out the two Amorite (a sayer) kings before you?, but not by your own sword or bow.
24.13: So I gave you? a land on which you did not toil and cities that you? did not build, and now you? live in them and eat from vineyards and olive groves that you? did not plant.
24.14: Now, therefore, fear the LORD {YHWH} and serve Him in sincerity and truth; cast aside the gods your? fathers served beyond the Euphrates and in Egypt (land of bondage), and serve the LORD.
24.15: But if it is unpleasing in your? sight to serve the LORD {YHWH}, then choose for yourselves this day whom you? will serve, whether the gods your? fathers served beyond the Euphrates, or the gods of the Amorites (sayers) in whose land you? are living. As for me and my house, we will serve the LORD!
24.16: The people replied, “Far be it from us to forsake the LORD {YHWH} to serve other gods!
24.17: For the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} brought us and our fathers out of the land of Egypt (land of bondage), out of the house of slavery, and performed these great signs before our eyes. He also protected us throughout our journey and among all the nations through which we traveled.
24.18: And the LORD {YHWH} drove out before us all the nations, including the Amorites (sayers) who lived in the land. We too will serve the LORD, because He is our God {Elohenu}!
24.19: But Joshua (YHWH saves) said to the people, “You? are not able to serve the LORD {YHWH}, for He is a holy God {Elohim}; He is a jealous God; He will not forgive your? rebellion or your? sins.
24.20: If you? forsake the LORD {YHWH} and serve foreign gods, He will turn and bring disaster on you? and consume you?, even after He has been good to you?.
24.21: “No! replied the people (YHWH saves). “We will serve the LORD {YHWH}!
24.22: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) told them, “You? are witnesses against yourselves that you? have chosen to serve the LORD {YHWH}. “We are witnesses! they said.
24.23: “Now, therefore,” he said, “get rid of the foreign gods among you? and incline your? hearts to the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God).
24.24: So the people said to Joshua (YHWH saves), “We will serve the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} and obey His voice.
24.25: On that day Joshua (YHWH saves) made a covenant for the people, and there at Shechem (shoulder) he established for them a statute and ordinance.
24.26: Joshua (YHWH saves) recorded these things in the Book of the Law of God {Elohim}. Then he took a large stone and set it up there under the oak (terebinth) that was near the sanctuary of the LORD {YHWH}.
24.27: And Joshua (YHWH saves) said to all the people, “You see this stone. It will be a witness against us, for it has heard all the words the LORD {YHWH} has spoken to us, and it will be a witness against you? if you? ever deny your? God {Elohim}.
24.28: Then Joshua (YHWH saves) sent the people away, each to his own inheritance.
24.29: Some time later, Joshua (YHWH saves) son of Nun (fish / posterity), the servant of the LORD {YHWH}, died at the age of 110.
24.30: And they buried him in the land of his inheritance, at Timnath-serah (portion of the sun) in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful), north of Mount Gaash (quaking).
24.31: Israel (he wrestles with God) had served the LORD {YHWH} throughout the days of Joshua (YHWH saves) and of the elders who outlived him and who had experienced all the works that the LORD had done for Israel.
24.32: And the bones of Joseph (YHWH has added), which the Israelites (God prevails) had brought up out of Egypt (land of bondage), were buried at Shechem (shoulder) in the plot of land that Jacob (he grasps the heel) had purchased from the sons of Hamor (he donkey), Shechem’s father, for a hundred pieces of silver \a hundred kesitahs/. So it became an inheritance for Joseph’s descendants.
24.33: Eleazar (God has helped) son of Aaron (light bringer) also died, and they buried him at Gibeah, which had been given to his son Phinehas (mouth of brass) in the hill country of Ephraim (doubly fruitful).
